concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Moscow leads Russia's urban system with 17.0% of tracked urban population across 1053 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Long-distance gravitational poles — Moscow & Saint Petersburg
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Moscow and Saint Petersburg (635 km apart, combined population 22,929,763). Long-distance connections dominate, with top pairs averaging 487 km apart — characteristic of large countries with geographically separated economic poles.

narration.synthesis: Russia shows moderate urban concentration: Moscow holds 17.0% of tracked population — significant but not overwhelming. The gravity network (Moscow → Saint Petersburg, avg 487 km) shows secondary cities maintaining real economic weight alongside the leading city.
